passages.title: Conformity
passages.source_html:
Conformity


A review of conformity and some of the studies that have been done on it


A


During your childhood, there will have been some kind of craze which affected all the people in your school. It may have been to do with a particular toy or possibly a must-have item of clothing. It may have been something as simple as a type of pen or as expensive as an electronic games console. Fashion designers, toy manufacturers and anyone else involved in the retail trade love conformity. Set up a craze, especially in the young and everyone will go for it. In fact, it's an ideal way to sell huge quantities of merchandise. The levels of conformity in consumerism are phenomenal. When you actually stand back and consider how easily we are persuaded that having certain items is the only way we can ensure peace of mind, you see what an important concept conformity is.


B 


Conformity has been described as 'yielding to group pressure' (Crutchfield1962). However, this implies that other people put pressure on us to make us conform and this is not always the case. A better definition is given by Aronson (1976) who said it was a 'change in a person's behaviour or opinions as a result of real or imagined pressure from a person or group of people.' This would make more sense, as often the pressure we feel is imagined. The person or group he refers to would have to be important to us at the time, regardless of their status.


C 


There has been considerable research on conformity. One of the first studies looked at the answers people gave when asked to estimate the number of beans in a bottle (Jennes,1932). If you have ever entered a 'guess the number' competition, you probably looked at the previous estimates made and based your judgment on what other people had guessed. This is more or less what happened in the Jennes study. First of all, he asked the respondents to give their own estimates, and then he asked them to decide a group estimate. Finally, he asked them alone again and discovered that they had stayed with the group answer.


D


 Probably the most famous study on conformity was undertaken by Asch (1951) when he created a situation where many of his subjects gave answers which were blatantly untrue, rather than contradict the people they were with. He did this by getting his subject to sit round a table with six stooges (colleagues of the experimenter) so that the subject was second to last. He showed them all a large card which had three lines of different lengths drawn on it, labelled A, B and C. He then gave them a card with a single line and asked them to match this in terms of length to one of the lines A, B or C.


E


 The stooges gave untrue responses in a number of the trials and the subjects were left in the situation where they either reported what they saw with their own eyes or conformed to the norm of the group. When the results were assessed, Asch found that in one out of every three trials where the wrong answer was given, the subject gave the same wrong answer as the stooges. This led to an average level of conformity of 32 per cent. Asch interviewed his subjects after the trials to try to find out why they conformed to an answer which was so obviously wrong. Most of them said that they did not want to cause problems within the group, although they also stated that when they did give wrong answers, it made them anxious. (Asch found that when there was just one other person present who did not go along with the majority, no matter how many it others there were, it was sufficient to make the subject give the right answer.)


F 


Kelman (1953) outlined three processes which can explain social conformity. The first is compliance where subjects go along with the crowd to prevent any in-group hostility or bad feeling and to maintain group harmony. However, they do not change their own private belief. If we look back to the Asch study, we can see that the subjects were simply complying with the demands of the experimental situation but hadn't actually internalized the group's norms. They agreed in public, but dissented in private. In a process known as internalization, however, subjects do actually see the view of the group as the more valid one. They may be able to do this, for example, by convincing themselves that their eyesight is poor. Sometimes, however, subjects actually seem to change their beliefs because they want to become more like their heroes. If they really want to become part of an in-group, they will start to identify with that group and take on the group's values and beliefs, even if they are different to their own. Kelman calls this identification. It frequently happens with teenagers who want to become more like a peer group in order to be accepted, and suddenly seem to go against all the values and beliefs of their parents.


G 


So why is it that we have to conform? Some people feel confident most of the time, have high self-esteem and do not have to go along with the majority. For most of us, though, how confident we feel varies from day to day, depending on the situation we are in, and this can influence behaviour.

groups.title: Questions 27-40
groups.source_html:
Questions 27 - 30


Choose YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer, choose NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer, or choose NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

27

Childhood crazes can center on items of any value.

正确答案： A. YES

题目意思：童年时期的流行可能集中在任何价值的物品上。  


定位关键信息：“childhood crazes”和“value”


对应原文：A段，“It may have been something as simple as a type of pen or as expensive as an electronic games console.”  


解题思路：原文提到流行的物品可以是“简单的笔”或者“昂贵的电子游戏机”，说明流行可以集中在任何价值的物品上。  

28

Children are more vulnerable to crazes now than they used to be.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

题目意思：现在的孩子比过去更容易受到流行趋势的影响。  


定位关键信息：“children”和“ vulnerable” 和“crazes”


对应原文：文章中没有明确提到现在的孩子比过去更容易受到流行的影响。

29

Consumers make too many quick decisions in shops.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

题目意思：消费者在商店里做出太多快速决策。  


定位关键信息：“consumers”和“quick decisions”  


对应原文：文章中没有提到消费者在商店里做出快速决策的内容。  


解题思路：原文讨论的是消费者在流行趋势中的从众行为，但没有涉及快速决策。  

30

Crutchfield's definition of conformity is the most reliable.

正确答案： B. NO

题目意思：Crutchfield对从众行为的定义是最可靠的。  


定位关键信息：“Crutchfield”和“most reliable”


对应原文：B段，“However, this implies that other people put pressure on us to make us conform and this is not always the case. A better definition is given by Aronson (1976) who said it was a 'change in a person's behaviour or opinions as a result of real or imagined pressure from a person or group of people.' This would make more sense, as often the pressure we feel is imagined. ”  


解题思路：原文指出Crutchfield的定义存在问题，并认为Aronson的定义更好，因此Crutchfield的定义不是最可靠的。

Questions 31 - 35


Complete the summary. Write ONE WORD ONLY in each gap.

31

Studies on conformity

In the Jennes study, people had to guess how many  were in a container. Jennes found that, in most cases, people opted for an estimate given by a . Asch asked his subjects to  line lengths. To test the extent to which people would conform, he placed his subjects with colleagues who gave  responses. He found that his subjects agreed with his colleagues 32% of the time, although they admitted to  feeling   about giving their answer.

正确答案： 31 beans  |  32 group  |  33 match  |  34 untrue  |  35 anxious
Question 31

题目意思：在Jennes的研究中，人们必须猜测容器里有多少……。  


定位关键信息：“Jennes study”  


对应原文：C段，“One of the first studies looked at the answers people gave when asked to estimate the number of beans in a bottle.”  


解题思路：原文明确提到研究的内容是估计瓶子里豆子的数量。

Question 32

题目意思：Jennes发现，大多数情况下，人们选择了由……给出的估计值。


定位关键信息：“Jennes”和“estimate”


对应原文：C段，“First of all, he asked the respondents to give their own estimates, and then he asked them to decide a group estimate. Finally, he asked them alone again and discovered that they had stayed with the group answer.”  


解题思路：原文提到人们在单独作答时，仍然保持与“group answer”一致，因此选择了群体提供的估计。

Question 33

题目意思：Asch要求他的被试者……线段长度。  


定位关键信息：“Asch”和“line lengths”


对应原文：D段，“He then gave them a card with a single line and asked them to match this in terms of length to one of the lines A, B or C.”


解题思路：原文提到Asch要求他们把这张卡片的长度与a、B或C中的一条线相匹配。

Question 34

题目意思：为了测试人们从众的程度，他把被试者和提供……答案的同事放在一起。


定位关键信息：“extent”和“colleagues ”和“responses”


对应原文：E段，“The stooges gave untrue responses in a number of the trials and the subjects were left in the situation where they either reported what they saw with their own eyes or conformed to the norm of the group.”  

解题思路：原文提到同事给出的答案是“untrue”。  

Question 35

题目意思：他发现他的被试者在32%的时间里同意了同事的答案，尽管他们承认对给出的答案感到……。

  

定位关键信息：“32%”和“feeling”


对应原文：E段，“ This led to an average level of conformity of 32 per cent. Asch interviewed his subjects after the trials to try to find out why they conformed to an answer which was so obviously wrong. Most of them said that they did not want to cause problems within the group. Although they also stated that when they did give it wrong answers made them anxious.”  


解题思路：原文提到被试者在给出错误答案时感到“anxious”。

Questions 36 - 40


Complete the table. Write ONE WORD ONLY in each gap.

36


Kelman's processes of social conformity

Compliance

• people support the majority view despite their own ideas

• social harmony is maintained

• illustrated by the results of the research 

  conducted by

• majority view is considered

   most  view

• people persuade themselves despite their own ideas

• people change their ideas to those of the majority

• typical of

正确答案： 36 Asch  |  37 Internalization  |  38 valid  |  39 Identification  |  40 teenagers
Question 36

题目意思：顺从的例子来自于谁的研究？ 

 

定位关键信息：“Kelman”和“Compliance” 


对应原文：F段，“If we look back to the Asch study, we can see that the subjects were simply complying with the demands of the experimental situation but hadn't actually internalized the group's norms.”  


解题思路：原文提到Asch的实验符合顺从（Compliance）的定义。

Question 37

题目意思：内化的特点是大多数观点被认为是……的。  


定位关键信息：“Kelman's processes”  和“majority view”


对应原文：F段，“They agreed in public, but dissented in private. In a process known as internalization, however, subjects do actually see the view of the group as the more valid one.”  


解题思路：原文提到他们在公开场合意见一致，但私下意见不同，这个过程被称为内化。  

Question 38

题目意思：大多数观点被认为是最……的观点。  


定位关键信息：“Kelman”和“Majority view”  


对应原文：F段，“They agreed in public, but dissented in private. In a process known as internalization, however, subjects do actually see the view of the group as the more valid one.”  


解题思路：原文中明确指出群体观点被认为是“valid”的。

Question 39

题目意思：人们改变观点以符合大多数的观点的过程被称为什么。


定位关键信息：“Kelman”和“people change their ideas to those of the majority”


对应原文：F段，“If they really want to become part of an in-group, they will start to identify with that group and take on the group's values and beliefs, even if they are different to their own. Kelman calls this identification.”  


解题思路：原文提到青少年为了被接受而进行同一化行为。 

Question 40

题目意思：同一化行为典型地出现在……中。  


定位关键信息：“Typical of”  


对应原文：F段，“It frequently happens with teenagers who want to become more like a peer group in order to be accepted, and suddenly seem to go against all the values and beliefs of their parents.”  


解题思路：原文明确提到青少年是典型的同一化行为群体。
